# Bangumi Page & Theme UX Improvements Design

Date: 2026-05-25
Scope: hexo-theme-reimu (local theme workspace)

## 1) Goals

This design addresses the requirements in `task.md`:

1. Fix bangumi cover resolution issues when using `source_url`, and unify bangumi data management in page front-matter.
2. Improve configuration clarity by moving user-customizable items to clearly documented config sections.
3. Make bangumi cards more compact and improve UI. Mandatory: remove `bangumi-card-note` and related configuration usage.
4. Remove the near-page-bottom whitening effect across interfaces. Must backup affected files before applying this change.
5. Replace `dark-mode-switch` visual style with the provided square-button sun/moon style while keeping existing theme color language and behavior.

Additional agreed constraint:
- Bilibili auto-fetch should be **experimental** and **fallback** to manual front-matter list when unavailable/failing.

## 2) Non-goals

- No new unrelated refactors.
- No dependency on private/authenticated Bilibili APIs.
- No guarantee of always-on Bilibili sync reliability; this is explicitly experimental.

## 3) Existing State Summary

Current relevant implementation:

- Bangumi rendering: `layout/bangumi.ejs`
- Bangumi styles: `source/css/_partial/bangumi.styl`
- Bangumi generation + metadata fetch/cache: `scripts/generator/bangumi.js`
- Dark mode switch markup: `layout/_partial/header.ejs`
- Dark mode switch styles: `source/css/_partial/header.styl`
- Dark mode behavior: `source/js/script.js`
- Theme config currently includes bangumi section and item examples in `_config.yml`

Observed issues:

- Cover URLs can be incorrect when metadata returns relative image paths.
- Bangumi items can originate from both page front-matter and theme config, creating ownership ambiguity.
- Card layout is oversized and includes redundant note text (`bangumi-card-note`).
- Whitening-at-bottom effect is perceived globally and should be removed.

## 4) Data Model & Ownership Design

### 4.1 Source of truth

`bangumi` page front-matter becomes the single content source of truth for bangumi entries.

Expected front-matter model (conceptual):

```yaml
---
layout: bangumi
title: 追番
bangumi:
  - title: ...
    source_url: ...
    cover: ...
    status: ...
    rating: ...
    episodes: ...
    year: ...
    tags: [...]
    summary: ...
    url: ...

bilibili:
  enable: true
  uid: "123456"
  mode: bangumi # reserved for future expansion
---
```

Theme `_config.yml` keeps only page-level display defaults (title/subtitle/description style settings if needed), but not concrete user bangumi items.

### 4.2 Generator priority & fallback

Generator resolution strategy:

1. If `page.bilibili.enable === true` and `uid` exists, try Bilibili experimental fetch.
2. If fetch succeeds and yields non-empty list, use fetched list.
3. Otherwise, fallback to `page.bangumi` list.
4. If still empty, render empty-state.

### 4.3 `source_url` cover fix

When metadata extraction returns a relative `og:image` / `twitter:image` path:

- Resolve against the final source page URL via URL resolution.
- Keep absolute `http/https` URLs unchanged.
- Avoid treating external URLs as local asset paths in template rendering.

Rendering rule:
- External URL: output as-is.
- Local relative path: pass through `url_for`.

## 5) Experimental Bilibili Fetch Design

### 5.1 Fetch behavior

- Implement in `scripts/generator/bangumi.js` as optional branch.
- Use public, unauthenticated endpoint strategy where available.
- Add short timeout and error swallowing similar to existing metadata fetch path.
- Normalize output to existing bangumi item schema.

### 5.2 Robustness

- Any network/API/parse failure should not break generation.
- Fallback to front-matter list silently.
- Cache fetched results in existing cache file namespace to limit repeated requests.

### 5.3 User-facing expectations

- Marked as experimental in config comments.
- Document that endpoint changes may degrade auto-fetch and fallback will be used.

## 6) UI/UX Design: Compact Bangumi Cards

### 6.1 Layout changes

- Reduce card padding and horizontal gap.
- Reduce cover width and cap cover-driven vertical growth.
- Keep 2-column desktop grid and 1-column mobile grid.
- Maintain readability via title hierarchy and compact meta pills.

### 6.2 Mandatory removals

Remove all `bangumi-card-note` usage:

- Remove note paragraph from card body.
- Remove footer static note text.
- Remove corresponding CSS class rules.
- Stop mapping/consuming `note`/`notes` in normalized item output.

### 6.3 Visual style constraints

- Keep theme palette coherence via existing CSS vars.
- Soften button/link visual weight to reduce card bulk.
- Preserve hover affordances without large glow footprint.

## 7) Bottom Whitening Effect Removal + Backup

### 7.1 Backup-first requirement

Before implementing whitening removal, create backup copies of all files to be changed for this step under:

`backup/2026-05-25-bottom-whitening/`

Back up exact source files prior to edits.

### 7.2 Effect removal strategy

- Identify global pseudo-elements/masks/gradients responsible for near-bottom whitening.
- Remove or neutralize only whitening-layer rules.
- Preserve background image, glass card, and existing dark/light theme behavior.

## 8) Dark Mode Switch Redesign

### 8.1 Markup structure

Replace current switch visual structure in `layout/_partial/header.ejs` with:

- hidden checkbox input (`#dark-mode-switch` retained for JS compatibility)
- square control label container
- two SVG icons (sun/moon) toggled by checked state

### 8.2 Behavioral compatibility

- Keep ID and interaction contract used by `source/js/script.js`.
- Keep keyboard focus styling and aria labeling.
- No logic changes required unless structure necessitates selector updates.

### 8.3 Styling adaptation

Implement style in `source/css/_partial/header.styl` using theme variables:

- match requested geometry and transition style
- map yellow/sky accents to existing red theme family and dark mode equivalents
- ensure size and alignment with nav icons

## 9) Configuration Clarity Improvements

In `_config.yml` and docs comments:

- Consolidate bangumi-related user knobs into clear section boundaries.
- Remove/avoid duplicate content-source config.
- Add explicit notes describing what belongs in page front-matter vs theme config.

## 10) Testing & Verification Strategy

1. Build/render validation:
   - Run Hexo generation command and ensure no exceptions.
2. Data path checks:
   - Manual bangumi list only.
   - `source_url` metadata fill with relative cover.
   - Bilibili experimental enabled success path.
   - Bilibili failure path fallback to manual list.
3. UI checks:
   - Bangumi card compactness on desktop/mobile.
   - `bangumi-card-note` absent from DOM.
   - Dark mode switch toggles correctly and persists mode.
   - Near-bottom whitening removed on representative pages.

## 11) Risks & Mitigations

- Bilibili endpoint instability: mitigate with fallback + cache + timeout.
- CSS regressions in header/nav: limit scope to switch class namespace.
- Over-removal of gradient effects: target only bottom-whitening selectors and verify visually.

## 12) Implementation Boundaries

This work should touch only relevant files:

- `layout/bangumi.ejs`
- `source/css/_partial/bangumi.styl`
- `scripts/generator/bangumi.js`
- `layout/_partial/header.ejs`
- `source/css/_partial/header.styl`
- `_config.yml`
- plus backup files for whitening step

No unrelated file churn.
